Mark Rice of McNeil Silveira Rice & Wiley, Tel: 415.472.3434, for plaintiffs Boris Berman, Svetlana M. Shirinova, Attorney at Law, Tel: 415.947.0703, for Defendants Leon Rader.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LEON RADER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to all five causes of action is: overruled as to all causes of action. Liberally construed, the FAC is not uncertain and adequately alleges all five causes of action for breach of contract, fraud by concealment, promissory fraud, intentional infliction of emotional distress, and violation of the UCL. As to breach of contract, the FAC adequately alleges the terms of the contract, Mr. Rader's breach, and damages. Construing the FAC liberally, it can be inferred that Mr. Berman performed his obligations under the contract or was excused from doing so. As to concealment, the FAC adequately alleges that Mr. Rader knew he was prohibited from providing headstones to Eternal Home Cemetery, Mr. Berman only learned this upon complaining to the cemetery, Mr. Rader failed to disclose the fact with deceptive and wrongful intent, Mr. Berman would not have done business with Mr. Rader had he known the fact, and Mr. Berman was harmed as a proximate result. As to false promise, the FAC adequately alleges that Mr. Rader promised to supply a headstone according to the specifications in the contract and Mr. Rader did not intend to perform because he knew he was prohibited from doing so by the cemetery. Construing the FAC liberally, it can be inferred that Mr. Rader intended for Mr. Berman to rely on the promise since the FAC states Mr. Rader knew Mr. Berman would not have purchased the headstone had he known Mr. Rader was prohibited from supplying it to the cemetery. It can also be inferred that Mr. Berman reasonably relied on the promise and that Mr. Rader did not perform because Mr. Berman alleges he had to make arrangements for a temporary headstone after Mr. Rader's noncompliance with the contract. The FAC adequately alleges extreme or outrageous conduct sufficient for an IIED claim, especially given the very personal nature of the headstone to Mr. Berman, which was known to Mr. Rader. The FAC adequately alleges a claim for violation of the UCL based on the alleged fraudulent business practices of concealing Mr. Rader's prohibition from Eternal Home Cemetery and disguising deliveries of headstones as if manufactured and sold by someone other than Mr. Rader. Mr. Rader's request to stay the UCL claim is not amenable to resolution on a demurrer, which only addresses the sufficiency of a pleading or portions thereof. Nor is Mr. Berman a party to the San Mateo case. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Boris Berman First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-16-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Rice, of McNeil, Silveira, Rice & Wiley, Tel. 415.472.3434, for plaintiffs Boris Berman,
Svetlana M. Shirinova, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.947.0703, for defendants Leon Rader.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LEON RADER's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Boris Berman First Amended Complaint is denied as to all portions of plaintiff Boris Berman's first amended complaint (FAC) sought to be stricken. The FAC adequately alleges entitlement to punitive damages based on the two fraud claims. The FAC adequately alleges entitlement to attorney's fees based on the parties' contract and Civil Code 1717. The request to strike the fifth cause of action and the request for injunctive and equitable relief alleged on page 10, lines 21 and 22 is denied. The UCL claim is adequately alleged notwithstanding the pendency of the San Mateo case. The motion to strike improperly seeks resolution of a factual issue as to the lawfulness of Eternal Home Cemetery's prohibition. Mr. Berman is not a party to the San Mateo lawsuit and has alleged that the prohibition existed at all relevant times. The issue of which court has priority to rule on injunctive relief as to the lawfulness of the prohibition is not amenable to resolution on a motion to strike, which only addresses the sufficiency of a pleading or portions thereof. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
